ocr.text: In the Q1 2024 report by Nutrien Ltd, the Farm Input Cost Indices table presents various agricultural inputs categorized under
Fertilizers, Pesticides, and Total Inputs. Under Fertilizers, Nitrogen holds a Cost Index of 150.5 with a Rating of High,
reflecting a Change of +3.2% and a Stability status of Stable.
Phosphorus follows with a Cost Index of 145.3, rated Moderate, showing a Change of +2.8% and also classified as Stable.
Potash has a Cost Index of 148.7, rated Moderate, with a Change of +1.5% and is Stable as well. Within the Pesticides category,
Herbicides start with a Cost Index of 130.2, rated Low, indicating a Change of -1.0% and a Declining stability. Atrazine
displays a Cost Index of 128.5, rated Low, with a Change of -0.5% and is Stable. Insecticides include Chlorpyrifos with a Cost
Index of 110.4, rated Moderate, showing a Change of +1.8% and Stable, while Neonicotinoids have a Cost Index of 112.7, also
rated Moderate, with a Change of +2.0% and Stable. Finally, the Total Inputs category culminates with a Cost Index of 135.7,
reflecting the overall agricultural input landscape. This detailed analysis of input costs captures essential shifts in agricultural
economics, providing stakeholders with critical insights into cost trends and stability across various input types.
